description_long: This is a dark gray, twin-engine military-style airplane with a straight, broad wingspan and a robust, angular fuselage. The engines are mounted high on the fuselage, just ahead of the tail, and feature prominent nacelles. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a clear canopy, providing wide visibility. The tail section is distinctive, with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall design is utilitarian and rugged, suitable for low-altitude, high-durability operations.
description: A dark gray, twin-engine military-style airplane with a straight wingspan, H-tail, and forward enclosed cockpit. The engines are mounted high on the fuselage, and the design is robust and angular.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, straight wings, forward cockpit, and twin engines mounted above the fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit canopy, and the high-mounted engine nacelles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the rear of the engine nacelles.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cockpit, wing, and engine placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Straight, low-wing configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted on pylons above the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single clear canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
